item.title: Exercise 2: Documentation Plan
item.dc:creator: pedro
item.description: The following plan was drawn up in discussion between Pedro, Elena and John. It sets out the methods we will use, who is responsible, and how material will be shared across the team. What Method...
item.content:encoded: The following plan was drawn up in discussion between Pedro, Elena and John. It sets out the methods we will use, who is responsible, and how material will be shared across the team.

item.title: Exercise 1: Documentation Phases
item.dc:creator: pedro
item.description: Phase 1 This phase covers everything from the first planning meeting through to the final rehearsals. The core question is: how do we capture a process that is spread across three people, multiple school departments,...
item.content:encoded:
Phase 1

This phase covers everything from the first planning meeting through to the final rehearsals. The core question is: how do we capture a process that is spread across three people, multiple school departments, and a group of 60 ten-year-old students?

Pedro’s learning journal will be the primary record. After each collaborative meeting, class visit, or composition session, I will write a short entry reflecting on what happened, what decisions were made, and what questions remain open. This is also what the OCA requires, so the journal serves both the course and the project.

Meeting notes will be written up after each formal meeting between Pedro, Elena and John. These do not need to be long. A few bullet points covering what was discussed, what was agreed, and what each person will do next.

Phase 2

This phase covers material we need to gather in order to make the work. Research, resources, and raw material that will inform or appear in the composition.

Historical research notes on Portuguese history from the 1950s to the present, gathered in collaboration with Portuguese language and history teachers at CAISL.

Audio references — recordings of Portuguese music from different decades (fado, José Afonso, Madredeus, contemporary artists) that will help Pedro understand the musical landscape he is composing within and give John and Elena reference points for the stylistic direction of each section.

Draft scores and sketches — each version of the score, however incomplete, will be saved and shared. This creates a record of how the composition evolved and shows where collaborator or student feedback caused a revision.

Phase 3

This phase covers the final work and its presentation in June.

A video recording of the performance will be made. This is the primary record of the finished piece and the students’ realisation of it.

A final annotated score will be produced after the performance, incorporating any changes made during rehearsals. This is the document that could be used if the piece were ever performed again.

A short written reflection on how the process unfolded and what they would do differently. These will be written independently and then shared, forming the basis of the project evaluation.

A selection of student responses, gathered informally after the performance, capturing how the students experienced the work.
